verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein expressed in the epididymis and secreted into the epididymal lumen. It binds to the sperm surface (postacrosomal/equatorial segment region) during epididymal transit, becoming stably associated with the sperm plasma membrane outer face — a wash-resistant, non-equilibrium association acquired during passage through the specialized secretory environment of the epididymis. This stable surface tethering to sperm is the canonical biology. However, this surface display is strictly restricted to the germline/reproductive lineage (epididymis-secreted protein binding sperm), making it tissue/cell-type restricted rather than broadly constitutive. This maps to contextual / tissue_restricted_surface. No broad somatic surface expression is documented.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Unclear whether CRISP1 binding to sperm surface is truly wash-resistant/covalent (stable_surface_attachment) versus reversible recruitment; the dominant mechanism is epididymis-to-sperm coating, which is germline-restricted either way.
